props.caption: China's open weights push the contest from best model to cheapest deployable one

items.note: Kyiv's toll from the 1-2 July barrage settled at 30 killed and 92 injured, its deadliest strike of the year, and the cost is now visibly two-way: Novorossiysk suspended retail gasoline sales and Anapa rationed fuel as Ukraine's strikes on Russian energy reached Black Sea pumps.

Brent slid to about $71.72 and the forward curve flipped into contango for the first time this year, the clearest sign the market has stopped pricing a Hormuz shortage. The Doha mediation stayed paused until after 9 July and IAEA inspectors had not returned to the struck Iranian sites.

Z.ai's open-weight GLM-5.2 and Meituan's domestic-chip LongCat-2.0 pressed on closed-model economics as US semiconductors sold off, while China's securities regulator cleared Unitree to raise about $619 million for a robot manufacturing base.

The UN Human Rights Council opened an urgent debate on 3 July over the human-rights situation in and around El Obeid, the North Kordofan junction linking Khartoum, Kordofan and Darfur, as warnings of mass-atrocity risk sharpened.

items.what: The EU's top court upheld Google's roughly €4.1 billion Android fine, preserving one of the bloc's largest competition penalties and its platform-regulation strategy.
The US declined to renew USMCA in its current form, keeping the pact in force but pushing it into rolling annual reviews; Washington and Mexico meet again the week of 20 July.
The Democratic Republic of the Congo began the first treatment trial against its Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak, an early test of whether the flare-up can be contained quickly.
Taiwan ran whole-of-society defence exercises in Nantou County built around a severe scenario of port disruption and mass casualties, involving more than 370 officials and civilians.
Spot gold rose about 1.3% to roughly $4,174 an ounce after the soft US payrolls print weakened the dollar, putting it on track for its first weekly gain in five weeks.
China's UBTech launched AI-powered companion robots, a line running from RMB 119,800 for torso-only units up to RMB 990,000 for full-size models.
